headline_rest: the constraint I found, and what changed.

case_studies.title: The Invisible Architecture-Review Queue
case_studies.description: Leadership blamed a "productivity problem." Six months of delivery data showed dev and test cycle times were fine — the real delay was a mandatory architecture-approval step nobody counted. I made it visible, added queue metrics and SLEs, and delegated low-risk decisions back to the teams.

case_studies.title: Kanban at Scale With Almost No Leverage
case_studies.description: Two coaches for an entire branch. I ran the coaching itself as a flow system — time-boxed engagements, internal champions, a distributed improvement network — and moved critical work out of notebooks and spreadsheets into a visible, governable system.

case_studies.title: Delivery Across an Enterprise You Don't Own
case_studies.description: Influence without authority across distributed teams, client and vendor stakeholders. I owned "the spaces between teams," surfacing cross-team dependencies early enough to manage rather than escalate — giving client executives one credible picture of delivery risk.

personal.title: This site
personal.description: An AI-built portfolio — Next.js static export, Tailwind v4, all content as JSON, built with Claude Code.

personal.title: Flow-analytics toolkit
personal.description: Bespoke delivery forecasting — Python and Jupyter pulling Jira/ADO APIs for Monte Carlo and throughput forecasting, independent of vendor tools.
